content: We can adapt the scalable shared retention memory architecture from multi-agent active learning to build a collaborative swarm of Computer-Using Agents (CUAs), where all agents share a unified, retention-optimized memory of interaction patterns across different computer interfaces and user tasks. This shared memory lets new individual CUAs zero-shot adapt to niche operating workflows without repeated reinforcement learning fine-tuning, while the scalable architecture avoids memory bloat as the agent swarm grows.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
